lede: A look at February 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alessandro Volta and Dr. Dre, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Revolution of Dignity
events.desc: Revolution of Dignity: At least 76 people are killed and hundreds are injured in clashes between riot police and demonstrators in Kyiv, Ukraine.

events.title: Brussels Airport diamond heist
events.desc: Armed robbers steal a haul of diamonds worth $50 million during a raid at Brussels Airport in Belgium.

events.title: WikiLeaks
events.desc: WikiLeaks publishes the first of hundreds of thousands of classified documents disclosed by the soldier now known as Chelsea Manning.

events.title: Federal Bureau of Investigation
events.desc: FBI agent Robert Hanssen is arrested for spying for the Soviet Union. He is ultimately convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment.

events.title: Sampit conflict
events.desc: Sampit conflict: Inter-ethnic violence between Dayaks and Madurese breaks out in Sampit, Central Kalimantan, Indonesia, ultimately resulting in more than 500 deaths and 100,000 Madurese displaced from their homes.

events.title: Xinjiang 61st Regiment Farm fire
events.desc: The Xinjiang 61st Regiment Farm fire started during Chinese New Year when a firecracker ignited memorial wreaths of the late Mao Zedong, killing 694 personnel. It remains the deadliest fireworks accident in the world.

events.title: Kenya
events.desc: Kenyan rebel leader Dedan Kimathi is executed by the British colonial government.

events.title: January
events.desc: While studying photographs taken in January, Clyde Tombaugh discovers Pluto.

events.title: Bulgaria
events.desc: Bulgarian revolutionary leader Vasil Levski is executed by hanging in Sofia by the Ottoman authorities.

events.title: Eighty Years' War
events.desc: Eighty Years' War: Off the coast of Cornwall, England, a Spanish fleet intercepts an important Anglo-Dutch merchant convoy of 44 vessels escorted by six warships, destroying or capturing 20 of them.

births.name: Dr. Dre
births.line: American rapper, record producer, and entrepreneur

deaths.text: Vasil Levski, Bulgarian activist, founded the Internal Revolutionary Organization
Martin Luther, German priest and theologian, leader of the Protestant Reformation

observances.text: Christian feast day: Simeon of Jerusalem (Western Christianity)
Independence Day, celebrates the independence of the Gambia from the United Kingdom in 1965
